title: Timeout Layers, Deadlines, Cancellation, Cleanup, and Budget Allocation · Go Client Systems · Spiral Systems Review
summary: A security reviewer reconstructing an expired incident handoff must reconstruct the governing policy from partial evidence after delay and distinguish current rules from tempting earlier ones for timeout layers, deadlines, cancellation, cleanup, and budget allocation, producing end-to-end request budget and cancellation model. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a Go 1.26 net/http end-to-end request budget and cancellation model after a delayed changed case. Learners progress throu…
objectives: Separate name resolution, connect, TLS, response-header, body-read, idle, and whole-operation timeout controls. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Propagate caller cancellation and deadlines through request creation, helpers, parsing, retries, and downstream work. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Allocate an end-to-end latency budget across attempts and phases while preserving time for cleanup and fallback. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Timeout Layers
steps.instruction:
Order the Timeout Layers Go HTTP evidence cycle for this review task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Go Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Timeout Layers.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Timeout Layers assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Go Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Timeout Layers, keep Timeout Layers connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.

content.text: Go security handoff client has 450 ms end-to-end for DNS, connect, TLS, headers, streamed body, parse, and cleanup. A caller cancels during body read; allocate phase budgets, propagate the reason, stop owned work, and preserve unknown remote effect. Competency probe: Separate name resolution, connect, TLS, response-header, body-read, idle, and whole-operation timeout controls. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Timeout Layers has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.
